templates.name: VMware Guest
templates.description:
Note: To enable trigger for free space for guest VM, set macro '{$VMWARE.VM.FS.TRIGGER.USED}' to the value '1' on the discovered host level.

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"

items.name: Cluster name
items.description: Cluster name of the guest VM.

items.name: Snapshot consolidation needed

dependencies.name: VMware Guest: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode

items.name: CPU latency in percent
items.description: Percentage of time the VM is unable to run because it is contending for access to the physical CPU(s).

items.name: Number of virtual CPUs
items.description: Number of virtual CPUs assigned to the guest.

items.name: CPU readiness latency in percent
items.description: Percentage of time that the virtual machine was ready, but was unable to get scheduled to run on the physical CPU.

items.name: CPU ready
items.description: Time that the VM was ready, but unable to get scheduled to run on the physical CPU during the last measurement interval (VMware vCenter/ESXi Server performance counter sampling interval - 20 seconds).

items.name: CPU swap-in latency in percent
items.description: Percentage of CPU time spent waiting for a swap-in.

items.name: CPU usage in percent
items.description: CPU usage as a percentage during the interval.

items.name: CPU usage
items.description: Current upper-bound on CPU usage. The upper-bound is based on the host the VM is current running on, as well as limits configured on the VM itself or any parent resource pool. Valid while the VM is running.

items.name: Datacenter name
items.description: Datacenter name of the guest VM.

items.name: Guest memory swapped
items.description: Amount of guest physical memory that is swapped out to the swap space.

items.name: Uptime of guest OS
items.description: Total time elapsed since the last operating system boot-up (in seconds). Data is collected if Guest OS Add-ons (VMware Tools) are installed.

parameters: Performance counter data is not available.

items.name: Hypervisor maintenance mode

triggers.name: VMware Guest: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode
triggers.event_name: VMware Guest: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode
triggers.description: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode. All other problem on the host will be suppressed.

items.name: Hypervisor name
items.description: Hypervisor name of the guest VM.

items.name: Ballooned memory
items.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is currently reclaimed through the balloon driver.

items.name: Compressed memory
items.description: The amount of memory currently in the compression cache for this VM.

items.name: Host memory consumed
items.description: Amount of host physical memory consumed for backing up guest physical memory pages.

items.name: Private memory
items.description: Amount of memory backed by host memory and not being shared.

items.name: Shared memory
items.description: The amount of guest physical memory shared through transparent page sharing.

items.name: Swapped memory
items.description: The amount of guest physical memory swapped out to the VM's swap device by ESX.

items.name: Guest memory usage
items.description: The amount of guest physical memory that is being used by the VM.

items.name: Host memory usage
items.description: The amount of host physical memory allocated to the VM, accounting for the amount saved from memory sharing with other VMs.

items.name: Memory size
items.description: Total size of configured memory.

items.name: Host memory usage in percent
items.description: Percentage of host physical memory that has been consumed.

items.name: Power state
Boot time
items.description: System boot time.

items.name: Snapshot count
items.description: Snapshot count of the guest VM.

items.name: Get snapshots
items.description: Snapshots of the guest VM.

items.name: Snapshot latest date
items.description: Latest snapshot date of the guest VM.

items.name: VM state

triggers.name: VMware Guest: VM is not running
triggers.description: VMware virtual machine is not running.

dependencies.name: VMware Guest: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode

items.name: Committed storage space
items.description: Total storage space, in bytes, committed to this VM across all datastores.

items.name: Uncommitted storage space
items.description: Additional storage space, in bytes, potentially used by this VM on all datastores.

items.name: Unshared storage space
items.description: Total storage space, in bytes, occupied by the VM across all datastores that is not shared with any other VM.

items.name: VMware Tools status
items.description:
Monitoring of VMware Tools. One of the following:
- Guest tools executing scripts: VMware Tools is starting.
- Guest tools not running: VMware Tools is not running.
- Guest tools running: VMware Tools is running.

triggers.name: VMware Guest: VMware Tools is not running
triggers.description: VMware Tools is not running on the VM.

dependencies.name: VMware Guest: Hypervisor is in the maintenance mode
VMware Guest: VM is not running

items.name: VMware Tools version
items.description: Monitoring of the VMware Tools version.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: System uptime.

item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine network interface input statistics (bytes per second).

item_prototypes.name: Number of packets received on interface [{#IFBACKINGDEVICE}]/[{#IFDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine network interface input statistics (packets per second).

item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine network interface output statistics (bytes per second).

item_prototypes.name: Number of packets transmitted on interface [{#IFBACKINGDEVICE}]/[{#IFDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine network interface output statistics (packets per second).

item_prototypes.name: Network utilization on interface [{#IFBACKINGDEVICE}]/[{#IFDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine network utilization (combined transmit and receive rates) during the interval.

item_prototypes.name: Average number of outstanding read requests to the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: Average number of outstanding read requests to the virtual disk during the collection interval.

item_prototypes.name: Average read latency from the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: The average time a read from the virtual disk takes.

item_prototypes.name: Average write latency to the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: The average time a write to the virtual disk takes.

item_prototypes.name: Average number of outstanding write requests to the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: Average number of outstanding write requests to the virtual disk during the collection interval.

item_prototypes.name: Average number of bytes read from the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine disk device read statistics (bytes per second).

item_prototypes.name: Average number of reads from the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine disk device read statistics (operations per second).

item_prototypes.name: Average number of bytes written to the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine disk device write statistics (bytes per second).

item_prototypes.name: Average number of writes to the disk [{#DISKDESC}]
item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine disk device write statistics (operations per second).

item_prototypes.description: VMware virtual machine file system statistics (percentage).

trigger_prototypes.name: VMware Guest: [{#FSNAME}]: Disk space is critically low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: VMware Guest: [{#FSNAME}]: Disk space is critically low (below {$VMWARE.VM.FS.PFREE.MIN.CRIT:"{#FSNAME}"}% for 5m)
trigger_prototypes.description: The disk free space on [{#FSNAME}] has been less than `{$VMWARE.VM.FS.PFREE.MIN.CRIT:"{#FSNAME}"}`% for 5m.

macros.description: If the hypervisor is in maintenance mode, all other problems on the VM will be suppressed automatically. Set to "1"/"0" to enable or disable this feature.
VMware service (vCenter or ESX hypervisor) SDK URL (https://servername/sdk).
VMware guest free space threshold for the critical trigger.
VMware guest free space threshold for the warning trigger.
VMware guest used free space trigger. Set to "1"/"0" to enable or disable the trigger.
